h2h_summary: Belgium and Senegal have never played against each other, resulting in zero wins, draws, or goals between the two teams.
key_players_away: Nicolas Jackson (striker, Bayern Munich loan from Chelsea)
team_style_home: Belgium under Rudi Garcia use a 4-2-3-1 emphasising possession, quick transitions after winning the ball, with Kevin De Bruyne as the creative engine and pacey wide attackers like Jeremy Doku exploiting space on the flanks.
team_style_away: Senegal play an energetic 4-3-3 built on high pressing, rapid wing transitions, and Sadio Mane's ability to drop deep and link play, combining physical intensity with technical flair from their European-based attackers.
wc_history_home: Belgium have participated in 15 World Cups and achieved their best-ever finish of third place at Russia 2018, where their Golden Generation featuring De Bruyne, Hazard, and Lukaku defeated England 2-0 in the third-place play-off.
wc_history_away: Senegal have appeared at three World Cups (2002, 2018, 2022), with their finest hour a stunning 2002 debut that knocked out defending champions France and reached the quarter-finals — their best-ever finish.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: World-class attacking quality with De Bruyne, Doku, Lukaku, and Trossard
Excellent goalkeeper in Thibaut Courtois (two-time UEFA Champions League winner with Real Madrid)
Strong squad depth and form — on a 13-match unbeaten run heading into the tournament
strengths_away: Star-studded attack with pace and physicality across the front three
Solid defensive record — conceded only three goals in African qualifying
AFCON 2021 and 2026 champions with tournament winning experience
